title: Peace Day for the World
story: Every August 6, people in Hiroshima, Japan, and around the world gather to remember those lost in 1945 and pray for peace. At exactly 8:15 in the morning, bells ring and everyone observes a moment of silence. Thousands of colorful paper cranes are folded and displayed because cranes symbolize hope and healing. People light floating lanterns on the river at night, creating a beautiful glow that honors the past while looking toward a peaceful future.
activity_question: What do paper cranes symbolize on Hiroshima Peace Memorial Day?
follow_up_question: What small actions can you take to help create peace in your community?

title: Float Your Way to Fizzy Fun!
story: Root beer floats get their own special day every August 6! People across the country celebrate by scooping vanilla ice cream into tall glasses and pouring bubbly root beer right over the top. The ice cream creates a foamy, creamy layer that looks like it's floating - that's how this tasty treat got its name! Families visit ice cream shops, diners fire up their soda fountains, and kids learn to make perfect floats at home. Some people get creative by trying different ice cream flavors or adding whipped cream and cherries on top.
activity_question: What creates the foamy layer on top of a root beer float?
activity_choices: The ice cream mixing with the root beer
activity_answer: The ice cream mixing with the root beer
bonus_fact: Root beer floats were invented in 1893 by a soda shop owner in Colorado!

title: Minty Fresh Fun Day!
story: People across the country celebrate National Fresh Breath Day every August 6 by focusing on keeping their mouths minty clean! Dentists love this holiday because it reminds everyone to brush their teeth extra well, floss carefully, and maybe try a new mouthwash flavor. Some people challenge friends to see who can keep their breath freshest all day long, while others stock up on sugar-free gum and breath mints to share.
activity_question: What do people focus on during National Fresh Breath Day?
activity_choices: Eating as much garlic as possible
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to keep your breath fresh throughout the day?

title: Wiggle Your Way to Happiness!
story: Your toes are about to become the stars of the show! National Wiggle Your Toes Day happens every August 6, and it's exactly what it sounds like. People everywhere wiggle their toes to celebrate these often-forgotten body parts. Some folks kick off their shoes at work or school and give their toes a good wiggle. Others post videos of their toe-wiggling skills on social media. Families have toe-wiggling contests to see who can move their toes the fastest or in the silliest way. It's a simple reminder to appreciate your feet and have some goofy fun!
activity_question: What do people often do with their shoes on National Wiggle Your Toes Day?
activity_choices: Kick them off to wiggle their toes
activity_answer: Kick them off to wiggle their toes
bonus_fact: Your toes have 26 bones, 33 joints, and over 100 muscles working together!
